title: The Peter-Penny.
content:
Fresh strewings allow
    To my sepulchre now,
    To make my lodging the sweeter;
    A staff or a wand
    Put then in my hand,
    With a penny to pay S. Peter.

    Who has not a cross
    Must sit with the loss,
    And no whit further must venture;
    Since the porter he
    Will paid have his fee,
    Or else not one there must enter.

    Who at a dead lift
    Can't send for a gift
    A pig to the priest for a roaster,
    Shall hear his clerk say,
    By yea and by nay,
    No penny, no paternoster.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "The Peter-Penny."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
